description: Six-digit Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP) code corresponding to the major or program of study at the sending institution. Enables standardized program comparison and transfer articulation across institutions.
reference: CIP (Classification of Instructional Programs) - National Center for Education Statistics (NCES); IPEDS

description: Ordinal rank of the student within their graduating class as reported on the transcript. Used in admissions criteria review for competitive programs. Null if the sending institution does not report class rank.
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act); AACRAO Standards

description: Total number of students in the graduating class at the sending institution, used in conjunction with class rank to calculate class rank percentile for admissions evaluation.
Timestamp when this transcript record was first created in the system, representing the point at which the transcript was logged into the admissions processing workflow.
reference: Ellucian Banner Admissions Module; FERPA Audit Requirements

description: Credit hour system used by the sending institution: semester hours, quarter hours, trimester hours, ECTS (European Credit Transfer System) credits, or institutional units. Required for accurate credit conversion during transfer evaluation.
reference: ECTS (European Credit Transfer System); AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: Total number of credit hours (CR) attempted by the student at the sending institution as reported on the transcript. Used in transfer credit evaluation and Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) determination.
reference: AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices; SAP (Satisfactory Academic Progress) Federal Regulations 34 CFR 668.34

description: Total number of credit hours (CR) successfully earned (passed) by the student at the sending institution as reported on the transcript. Distinct from credits attempted; used to assess academic completion rate.
reference: AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices; SAP (Satisfactory Academic Progress) Federal Regulations 34 CFR 668.34

description: Number of credit hours from the sending institution that have been approved for transfer to the receiving institution following evaluation. May be less than or equal to credits earned.
reference: AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices; Ellucian Banner Academic History

description: Cumulative Grade Point Average (GPA) as reported on the transcript from the sending institution, typically on a 4.0 scale. Used as a primary academic performance indicator in admissions criteria review and transfer credit eligibility assessment.
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act); AACRAO Standards; Ellucian Banner Academic History

description: Name of the degree, diploma, or credential awarded by the sending institution as printed on the transcript (e.g., Bachelor of Science, Associate of Arts, High School Diploma, GED). Null if no degree was conferred.
reference: CIP (Classification of Instructional Programs); IPEDS Completions Survey; AACRAO Standards

description: Method by which the transcript was delivered to the receiving institution: electronic (e.g., via Parchment, National Student Clearinghouse), physical mail, hand-delivered by applicant, or fax.
reference: AACRAO Electronic Transcript Standards; Parchment/National Student Clearinghouse EDI Standards

description: Indicates whether the transcript contains a disciplinary notation or academic integrity violation record from the sending institution (True). Used in admissions character review and compliance with institutional conduct policies.
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act); AACRAO Standards

description: External reference or tracking number assigned by the sending institution or transcript delivery service (e.g., Parchment order ID, NSC tracking number) to uniquely identify the transcript document for reconciliation and audit purposes.
reference: Parchment EDI Standards; National Student Clearinghouse; AACRAO Electronic Transcript Standards

description: Electronic transcript exchange network or delivery platform through which the transcript was received (e.g., Parchment, National Student Clearinghouse, Scrip-Safe, paper mail). Used for document provenance tracking and audit.
reference: AACRAO Electronic Transcript Standards; Parchment EDI Standards; National Student Clearinghouse

description: Calendar date on which the transcript evaluation was completed by the admissions evaluator or transfer credit analyst.
reference: Ellucian Banner Admissions Module; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: Current workflow status of the transcript evaluation process: pending (received, not yet reviewed), in_review (actively being evaluated), evaluated (review complete), accepted (credits or credentials accepted), rejected (not accepted), or on_hold (awaiting additional information). Drives admissions and transfer credit workflows.
reference: Ellucian Banner Admissions Module; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

business_glossary_term: Federal Interagency Committee on Education (FICE) Code
description: Six-digit Federal Interagency Committee on Education (FICE) code assigned by the U.S. Department of Education to identify postsecondary institutions. Used for IPEDS reporting and transfer credit institution matching.
reference: IPEDS (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System); U.S. Department of Education

description: Name of the NACES-member credential evaluation agency (e.g., WES, ECE, AACRAO EDGE) that evaluated the foreign transcript. Populated only when foreign_credential_eval_required is True.
reference: NACES (National Association of Credential Evaluation Services); AACRAO EDGE

description: Date on which the foreign credential evaluation was completed by the designated evaluation agency. Used to track evaluation turnaround and application completeness for international applicants.
reference: NACES (National Association of Credential Evaluation Services); AACRAO Standards

description: Indicates whether this transcript requires a foreign credential evaluation by a NACES-member evaluation service (True) because it originates from a non-U.S. institution. Triggers the foreign credential evaluation workflow in admissions.
reference: NACES (National Association of Credential Evaluation Services); U.S. Department of Education; AACRAO Standards

description: Maximum GPA scale used by the sending institution (e.g., 4.0, 5.0, 10.0, 100.0). Required to normalize and compare GPA values across institutions with different grading systems.
reference: AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices; ECTS (European Credit Transfer System) Guidelines

description: Date on which the student graduated or completed their program at the sending institution as reported on the transcript. Used to verify degree completion for graduate admissions and transfer eligibility.
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act); AACRAO Standards

description: Academic honors designation printed on the transcript from the sending institution (e.g., cum laude, magna cum laude, summa cum laude, honors program). Used in merit scholarship evaluation and admissions recognition.
reference: AACRAO Standards; Ellucian Banner Academic History

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country where the sending institution is located. Used to identify international transcripts requiring foreign credential evaluation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; NACES (National Association of Credential Evaluation Services)

description: Full legal name of the prior institution (high school, college, or university) that issued this official or unofficial transcript.
reference: IPEDS (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System); National Center for Education Statistics (NCES)

description: Two-letter U.S. state or territory abbreviation where the sending institution is located. Applicable for domestic institutions; null for international institutions.
Primary academic major or field of study as reported on the transcript from the sending institution. Used in transfer credit articulation and program admission requirements review.
reference: CIP (Classification of Instructional Programs); IPEDS; AACRAO Standards

description: Free-text notes entered by the admissions evaluator or registrar staff regarding special circumstances, exceptions, or observations related to this transcript evaluation. May contain sensitive academic information.
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act); Ellucian Banner Admissions Module

description: Indicates whether the transcript is an official document received directly from the issuing institution (True) or an unofficial copy submitted by the applicant (False). Official transcripts are required for final admissions decisions and enrollment confirmation.
reference: AACRAO (American Association of Collegiate Registrars and Admissions Officers) Standards; Ellucian Banner Admissions

description: Calendar date on which the transcript was received by the institution's admissions or registrar office. Used to track application completeness and compliance with admissions deadlines.
reference: Ellucian Banner Admissions Module; AACRAO Standards

description: End date of the last term of enrollment at the sending institution as reported on the transcript. Used to establish the duration of prior enrollment and recency of academic activity.
reference: AACRAO Standards; Ellucian Banner Academic History

description: Start date of the first term of enrollment at the sending institution as reported on the transcript. Used to establish the duration of prior enrollment and academic history timeline.
reference: AACRAO Standards; Ellucian Banner Academic History

description: Classification of the transcript by the level of education it represents: high school (secondary), undergraduate (bachelor's-level), graduate (master's/doctoral), professional (law/medical/business), or vocational/technical. Drives transfer credit evaluation rules and admissions criteria review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high_school|undergraduate|graduate|professional|vocational|international — promote to reference product]
reference: CIP (Classification of Instructional Programs); AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices; Ellucian Banner Academic History

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this transcript record, supporting audit trail requirements and change tracking for FERPA compliance and accreditation reporting.
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act); HLC Accreditation Standards; Ellucian Banner

description: Date on which the transcript was verified as authentic by the admissions office or third-party verification service. Populated only when verified_flag is True.
reference: HLC (Higher Learning Commission) Accreditation Standards; AACRAO Standards

description: Indicates whether the authenticity of the transcript has been independently verified by the admissions office or a third-party verification service (True). Supports fraud prevention and accreditation compliance.
reference: HLC (Higher Learning Commission) Accreditation Standards; SACSCOC Standards; AACRAO Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each transfer credit evaluation record in the Ellucian Banner Student Information System (SIS). Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete academic evaluation event with its own lifecycle.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module

description: Foreign key linking to enrollment.admission_decision. Business justification: Transfer credit evaluations directly inform and are referenced by admission decisions for transfer applicants. Linking transfer_credit_eval to the admission_decision that used it enables traceability

Reference to a formal articulation agreement between the receiving institution and the sending institution, if one exists. When populated, the evaluation follows pre-negotiated equivalency mappings rather than individual review.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: Reference to the faculty member, academic advisor, or registrar staff member who rendered the articulation decision. Satisfies PARTY_REFERENCE category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module; Workday HCM

description: Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_applicant. Business justification: Transfer credit evaluations are performed for transfer applicants. While transfer_credit_eval already links to enrollment_application, a direct FK to enrollment_applicant enables applicant-level trans
Reference to the admissions application submitted by the transfer applicant. Links the credit evaluation to the applicant's enrollment application record in Ellucian Banner Admissions.
Reference to the receiving institution's course catalog entry that the sending course has been articulated to. Null if no direct equivalent exists and credit is awarded as elective or generic credit.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Course Catalog Module

description: Reference to the academic department responsible for reviewing and rendering the articulation decision for this transfer course. Departments evaluate subject-matter equivalency.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Academic Structure Module

description: Reference to the student or prospective student record for whom the transfer credit evaluation is being conducted. May be populated after matriculation when the applicant becomes an enrolled student.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Student Records Module; FERPA

description: Foreign key linking to aid.sap_evaluation. Business justification: Transfer credits impact SAP maximum timeframe calculations (attempted hours vs. program length). Operational link for aid eligibility when transfer students approach 150% completion threshold. Require

Foreign key linking to enrollment.transcript. Business justification: Transfer credit evaluation is based on coursework documented in a specific transcript. This FK links the evaluation to the source transcript, enabling transcript-to-credit-award traceability. Removes

Outcome of the formal appeal process for a disputed transfer credit evaluation. 'upheld' = original denial maintained; 'overturned' = credit awarded upon appeal; 'pending' = appeal under review; 'withdrawn' = applicant withdrew appeal.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module; FERPA

description: Indicates whether the applicant or student has filed a formal appeal of the transfer credit evaluation decision (True = appeal filed; False = no appeal). Triggers appeal workflow in the registrar's office.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module; FERPA

description: Number of credit hours (CR) granted by the receiving institution for this transfer course after evaluation. May differ from sending_credit_hours due to unit conversion or partial credit awards. Satisfies QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category for this non-monetary transaction.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: Academic level classification of the sending institution's course. Determines how credit applies toward degree requirements (e.g., lower-division credits may not satisfy upper-division requirements). Critical for degree audit and program planning.
reference: AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices; Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module

description: Timestamp when this transfer credit evaluation record was first created in the system. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.

Name of the third-party credential evaluation service used to assess international transcripts (e.g., 'WES', 'ECE', 'NACES member'). Applicable only for foreign transcripts requiring external evaluation before institutional review.
reference: NACES (National Association of Credential Evaluation Services); U.S. Department of Education

description: Indicates whether academic credit was awarded for this transfer course (True = credit awarded; False = credit denied). Primary decision indicator used in transfer admission decisions and degree audit processing.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: Classification of how the awarded credit applies at the receiving institution. 'direct_equivalent' = maps to a specific course; 'elective' = counts as free elective; 'generic' = counts in subject area without specific course match; 'remedial' = developmental credit, not degree-applicable; 'no_credit' = denied; 'advanced_standing' = exemption without credit.
reference: AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices; Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module

description: Indicates the specific degree program(s) or major(s) for which this transfer credit evaluation applies (e.g., 'B.S. Computer Science', 'B.A. Psychology'). A course may satisfy requirements in one program but not another.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Degree Audit Module; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: Reason code explaining why transfer credit was denied or not awarded. Populated when credit_awarded_flag is False. Used for applicant communication, appeal processing, and policy analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: insufficient_grade|no_equivalent|outdated_content|unaccredited_institution|incomplete_documentation|remedial_course|other — promote to reference product]
reference: AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices; Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module

description: The date on which the evaluating department rendered its articulation decision. Represents the principal real-world business event timestamp (day-level) for this evaluation. Satisfies BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: Free-text notes entered by the evaluating department or registrar staff documenting the rationale for the articulation decision, conditions attached to conditional approvals, or instructions for the applicant. Subject to FERPA access controls.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module; FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act)

description: Human-readable, externally communicable reference number assigned to this evaluation record (e.g., TCE-2024-000123). Used in correspondence with applicants, advisors, and sending institutions. Satisfies BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module

description: Current lifecycle state of the transfer credit evaluation. 'pending' = submitted, awaiting review; 'in_review' = under departmental review; 'approved' = credit awarded; 'denied' = credit not awarded; 'conditional' = credit awarded pending additional documentation; 'withdrawn' = applicant withdrew request. Satisfies LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: Method by which the transfer credit evaluation was conducted. 'individual_review' = evaluated on a case-by-case basis by the department; 'articulation_agreement' = governed by a formal bilateral agreement; 'statewide_policy' = governed by state-mandated transfer policy; 'national_policy' = governed by national standards (e.g., AP, CLEP).
reference: AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices; U.S. Department of Education

description: The academic term for which the transfer applicant intends to enroll at the receiving institution (e.g., 'Fall 2024'). Used to align credit evaluation with the applicable catalog year and degree requirements.

The minimum letter grade that must have been earned at the sending institution for this course to be eligible for credit transfer under institutional policy (e.g., 'C', 'C-', 'B'). Used to enforce grade threshold policies during evaluation.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: Date on which the awarded transfer credit was officially posted to the student's academic transcript. Null until posting occurs. Used for enrollment timeline tracking and degree audit activation.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Academic History Module; FERPA

description: Indicates whether the awarded transfer credit has been officially posted to the student's academic transcript in the Student Information System (SIS) (True = posted; False = not yet posted). Credit is typically posted after official transcript receipt and matriculation.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Academic History Module; FERPA; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

business_glossary_term: Sending Course Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP) Code
description: Six-digit CIP (Classification of Instructional Programs) code assigned to the sending institution's course, used to classify the academic discipline for articulation matching, IPEDS reporting, and program alignment analysis.
reference: NCES CIP Code Taxonomy; IPEDS (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System)

description: The course number as recorded on the official transcript from the sending institution (e.g., 'MATH 201'). Preserved verbatim for audit and articulation agreement matching.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: The official course title as it appears on the sending institution's transcript (e.g., 'Calculus II'). Used by evaluators to assess subject-matter equivalency against the receiving institution's catalog.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Transfer Articulation Module; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: Number of credit hours (or equivalent units) assigned to the course at the sending institution as recorded on the official transcript. May differ from awarded credit hours due to unit conversion (e.g., quarter-to-semester conversion).
reference: AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices; ECTS (European Credit Transfer System) for international transcripts

description: The credit unit system used by the sending institution. Required for accurate credit hour conversion when the sending institution uses a different academic calendar system (e.g., quarter hours must be converted to semester hours at a 2/3 ratio).
reference: AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices; ECTS (European Credit Transfer System)

description: The letter grade or grade symbol earned in the course at the sending institution as recorded on the official transcript (e.g., 'A', 'B+', 'C', 'P', 'S'). Used to determine grade threshold eligibility for credit transfer under institutional policy.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Academic History Module; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: Numeric grade point value corresponding to the grade earned at the sending institution on that institution's grading scale (e.g., 3.700 for A-). Used for GPA (Grade Point Average) equivalency analysis and transfer GPA calculations.
reference: AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices; Ellucian Banner SIS — Academic History Module

description: Accreditation classification of the sending institution at the time of transcript evaluation. Determines eligibility for credit transfer under institutional policy. Regionally accredited institutions typically receive full credit consideration.
reference: U.S. Department of Education — Accreditation Database; Regional Accreditation Agencies (HLC; SACSCOC; MSCHE; NWCCU; NECHE; WSCUC)

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this transfer credit evaluation record originated. 'banner' = Ellucian Banner SIS; 'slate' = Slate CRM (preliminary evaluation during admissions); 'manual' = manually entered by registrar staff.

The academic term and year in which the course was completed at the sending institution as recorded on the transcript (e.g., 'Fall 2022', 'Spring 2023'). Used to assess course recency policies and catalog year applicability.
reference: Ellucian Banner SIS — Academic History Module; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: Indicates the form in which the sending institution's transcript was received. Official transcripts (paper or electronic) are required for final credit posting; unofficial or self-reported transcripts may be used for preliminary evaluation during admissions.
reference: AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices; Ellucian Banner SIS — Admissions Module

description: Timestamp when this transfer credit evaluation record was last modified. Tracks revisions to articulation decisions, credit awards, or equivalent course mappings. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
Unique surrogate identifier for each admissions waitlist record. Primary key for the waitlist data product in the enrollment domain.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module

description: Reference to the academic program (degree, certificate, or credential) for which the applicant is waitlisted. Supports capacity management by program.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP)

description: Foreign key linking to enrollment.admission_decision. Business justification: Admissions waitlist entry is created as the result of a waitlist admission decision. This FK links the waitlist record to the decision that placed the applicant on the waitlist, enabling tracking of d

Foreign key linking to enrollment.admission_offer. Business justification: When a waitlisted applicant is eventually offered admission, the waitlist record should reference the resulting admission_offer. The waitlist already has admission_decision_id (the initial waitlist de
Reference to the admissions counselor or recruiter assigned to manage this waitlist record and communicate with the applicant. Supports workload tracking and relationship management in Slate CRM.
reference: Slate CRM - Prospect Management; Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module

description: Reference to the applicant who has been placed on the admissions waitlist. Links to the applicant master record in the Student Information System.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; Slate CRM - Application Processing

description: Reference to the specific admissions application associated with this waitlist placement. An applicant may have multiple applications across terms or programs.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; Slate CRM - Application Processing

description: Reference to the academic entry term for which the applicant is waitlisted (e.g., Fall 2025, Spring 2026). Drives term-based enrollment cycle management.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Registration Module; IPEDS (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System)

description: Reference to the academic department responsible for the program for which the applicant is waitlisted. Enables department-level waitlist reporting and capacity oversight.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module

description: Reference to the college or school within the institution that administers the program for which the applicant is waitlisted (e.g., College of Engineering, College of Business). Supports college-level capacity management.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; AACSB; ABET

description: Level of study for which the applicant is waitlisted. Drives admissions workflow routing, capacity pool segmentation, and IPEDS reporting. Valid values: undergraduate, graduate, doctoral, professional, certificate.
reference: IPEDS (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System); U.S. Department of Education; Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module

description: Applicant's response to the waitlist placement notification indicating whether they wish to remain on the waitlist. Valid values: accepted (applicant confirmed intent to remain), declined (applicant declined waitlist placement), no_response (no response received by deadline).
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; Slate CRM - Application Processing

description: Date on which the applicant submitted their response (accept or decline) to the waitlist placement offer. Null if no response has been received.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; Slate CRM - Application Processing

description: Total number of seats available in the waitlist pool for the program and entry term at the time of this record's creation. Used in yield contingency planning to determine how many waitlisted applicants may be admitted.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; IPEDS

description: Six-digit CIP code assigned to the academic program for which the applicant is waitlisted. Used for IPEDS reporting, accreditation documentation, and program-level enrollment analytics.
reference: Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP) - National Center for Education Statistics (NCES); IPEDS

description: Applicant's citizenship classification relevant to admissions processing, financial aid eligibility, and IPEDS reporting. Valid values: us_citizen, permanent_resident, international, daca, unknown. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: us_citizen|permanent_resident|international|daca|unknown — promote to reference product]
reference: IPEDS (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System); U.S. Department of Education Title IV; FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act)

description: Applicant's preferred communication channel for waitlist-related notifications and correspondence. Drives outreach strategy in Slate CRM. Valid values: email, portal, mail, phone.
reference: Slate CRM - Communications; FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act)

description: Timestamp when this waitlist record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation marker for data lineage and compliance tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; FERPA; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Audit Standards

description: Classification of the applicant's entry pathway into the institution. FTIAC (First-Time-In-Any-College) indicates a first-time freshman; transfer indicates a student transferring from another institution. Supports cohort-based capacity planning and yield modeling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ftiac|transfer|readmit|international|non_degree — promote to reference product]
reference: IPEDS (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System); Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module

description: Indicates whether the applicant has a FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act) release authorization on file permitting disclosure of admissions and waitlist information to designated third parties (e.g., parents, guardians). True = release on file; False = no release on file.
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act); U.S. Department of Education

description: Ultimate outcome of the waitlist record at the close of the admissions cycle. Valid values: admitted (admitted from waitlist), denied (denied from waitlist by institution), withdrew (applicant withdrew from waitlist), no_action (waitlist closed with no action taken). Supports IPEDS reporting and yield analysis.
reference: IPEDS (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System); U.S. Department of Education; Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module

description: Date on which the final disposition of the waitlist record was recorded (admission, denial, withdrawal, or no action). Marks the closure of the waitlist lifecycle for this applicant-program-term combination.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; IPEDS

description: Indicates the financial aid eligibility status for the applicant if admitted from the waitlist. Waitlist admissions may have reduced or no institutional aid availability depending on remaining aid budget. Valid values: eligible (institutional aid available), not_eligible (no institutional aid remaining), pending_review (aid availability under review).
reference: U.S. Department of Education Title IV; FAFSA (Free Application for Federal Student Aid); Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Financial Aid Module

description: Applicant's intended enrollment intensity if admitted from the waitlist. Used in FTE (Full-Time Equivalent) projections and capacity planning. Valid values: full_time, part_time.
reference: IPEDS (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System); Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Registration Module

description: Indicates whether the institution has disclosed the applicant's waitlist position to the applicant. Some institutions do not disclose ranked positions. True = position disclosed to applicant; False = position not disclosed.
reference: U.S. Department of Education Admissions Transparency Guidance; Slate CRM - Communications

description: Indicates whether the waitlist for this program and term is a ranked (ordered) waitlist or an unranked pool. True = ranked waitlist with assigned positions; False = unranked pool where all applicants have equal consideration.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; U.S. Department of Education

description: Free-text field for admissions staff to record relevant observations, special circumstances, or follow-up actions related to this waitlist record. Subject to FERPA access controls.
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act); Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module

description: Date on which the applicant was formally notified of their waitlist placement via official communication (email, portal, or letter). Supports compliance with institutional notification timelines.
reference: Slate CRM - Communications; U.S. Department of Education Title IV Admissions Notification Requirements

description: Date on which the applicant formally accepted the admission offer extended from the waitlist. Null if offer was declined, not yet responded to, or not yet extended.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; Slate CRM - Application Processing

description: Date on which an admission offer was formally extended to the applicant from the waitlist. Null if no offer has been made. Distinct from the initial waitlist notification date.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; Slate CRM - Communications

description: Deadline date by which the applicant must accept or decline the admission offer extended from the waitlist. Supports yield management and seat reallocation planning.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; U.S. Department of Education Admissions Guidance

description: Total number of applicants on the waitlist for the same program and entry term at the time this record was created. Provides context for the applicant's relative position and probability of admission.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module

description: Ordinal rank of the applicant on the waitlist for the given program and entry term. Lower numbers indicate higher priority for admission offers. Used in yield contingency planning and capacity management.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module

description: IPEDS unit ID or FICE code of the most recent institution attended by the applicant prior to this application. Relevant for transfer applicants on the waitlist. Supports transfer credit evaluation and articulation agreement analysis.
reference: IPEDS (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System); U.S. Department of Education

description: Narrative or coded reason explaining why the applicant was placed on the waitlist rather than admitted or denied outright. Examples include capacity constraints, incomplete file pending review, or competitive pool evaluation. Supports admissions process transparency and appeals.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; Slate CRM - Application Processing

description: Applicant's residency classification for tuition and enrollment purposes. Affects tuition rate assignment and state-level enrollment reporting. Valid values: in_state, out_of_state, international.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; IPEDS; U.S. Department of Education

description: Deadline date by which the applicant must respond to the waitlist placement notification. Applicants who do not respond by this date may be removed from the waitlist.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; U.S. Department of Education Admissions Guidance

description: Indicates whether the applicant, if admitted from the waitlist, would be eligible for merit-based scholarship consideration. True = eligible for scholarship review upon waitlist admission; False = not eligible.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Financial Aid Module; U.S. Department of Education Title IV

description: Channel through which the waitlist placement was initiated or the applicant's response was received. Supports process analytics and system integration auditing. Valid values: online_portal, paper, slate_crm, edi, staff_entry.
reference: Slate CRM - Application Processing; Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this waitlist record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and ETL (Extract Transform Load) reconciliation in the Silver Layer. Valid values: banner (Ellucian Banner SIS), slate (Slate CRM), manual (manually entered).
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System; Slate CRM; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage Standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this waitlist record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Silver Layer.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Admissions Module; FERPA; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Audit Standards

description: Calendar date on which the applicant was officially placed on the admissions waitlist. Serves as the principal business event date for this record and is used to determine waitlist seniority when positions are equal.

reference: FAFSA; 34 CFR §668; U.S. Department of Education Title IV

description: Indicates whether the student is a first-generation college student (neither parent holds a bachelor's degree). Used for Title III/IV reporting, TRIO program eligibility, and institutional equity analytics.
reference: Higher Education Act Title IV; TRIO Programs; IPEDS

description: Student's Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) value for the term, calculated as credit hours attempted divided by the full-time credit hour threshold (typically 12 for undergraduate, 9 for graduate). Used for state funding formulas, IPEDS FTE reporting, and institutional resource planning.
reference: IPEDS Glossary; U.S. Department of Education; NACUBO

description: Indicates whether the student is a First-Time-In-Any-College (FTIAC) student. Required for IPEDS 12-Month Enrollment and Graduation Rate surveys and federal cohort default rate calculations.
reference: IPEDS; U.S. Department of Education; 34 CFR §668

description: Indicates whether the student holds a non-immigrant visa status (e.g., F-1, J-1) for this term. Drives SEVIS reporting requirements, international student services, and IPEDS international student enrollment counts.
reference: SEVIS (Student and Exchange Visitor Information System); DHS; IPEDS

description: The last date on which the student attended class or participated in an academically related activity during the term. Required for Return to Title IV (R2T4) calculations when a student withdraws and for federal financial aid compliance.
reference: 34 CFR §668.22 Return to Title IV (R2T4); U.S. Department of Education

description: Total cumulative grade quality points earned across all terms at the institution as of the end of this term. Used as the numerator in cumulative GPA calculation.
Total grade quality points earned during the term, calculated as the sum of (grade points × credit hours) for each course. Used as the numerator in term GPA calculation.

Indicates whether the student has an active registration hold at the time of this term record snapshot. Holds may be academic (probation), financial (outstanding balance), or administrative. Used for enrollment management and student services workflows.
Student's tuition residency classification for the term as determined by the institution's residency policy. Drives tuition rate assessment, state funding formulas, and IPEDS residency reporting.
reference: Ellucian Banner SGBSTDN; State Higher Education Finance Policy

description: Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) status for the term as required by Title IV federal financial aid regulations. Evaluates qualitative (GPA), quantitative (pace/completion rate), and maximum timeframe standards. Determines continued financial aid eligibility.
reference: 34 CFR §668.34; U.S. Department of Education Title IV SAP

description: Operational system of record from which this student term record was sourced. Primarily Ellucian Banner (SGBSTDN/SHRTTRM) for enrollment domain. Supports data lineage and ETL audit in the lakehouse.

Broad academic level of the student for the term (undergraduate, graduate, professional, doctoral, non-degree). Distinct from class level — drives tuition rate schedules, financial aid programs, and accreditation reporting.

Student's Grade Point Average (GPA) calculated for the current term only, based on quality points earned divided by credit hours attempted with grade points. Protected under FERPA. Used for academic standing determination and Dean's List eligibility.
reference: Ellucian Banner SHRTTRM; FERPA 34 CFR Part 99; AACRAO

description: Total credit hours accepted from prior institutions via transfer credit evaluation. Included in cumulative hours for degree audit and class level but may be excluded from institutional GPA calculation per policy.
reference: Ellucian Banner SHRTTRM; AACRAO Transfer Credit Practices

description: Timestamp when this student term record was last updated in the Silver Layer lakehouse, reflecting grade changes, academic standing recalculations, or SAP status updates post-term.
reference: Institutional Data Governance Policy; FERPA 34 CFR Part 99

description: Indicates whether the student is a veteran or active-duty military member. Drives VA education benefits processing (GI Bill), Yellow Ribbon Program eligibility, and veteran services reporting.
reference: 38 U.S.C. Chapter 33 (Post-9/11 GI Bill); VA School Certifying Official

description: Non-immigrant visa classification for international students. Required for SEVIS compliance reporting and determination of financial aid eligibility. Null for domestic students. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: F-1|J-1|M-1|H-1B|H-4|O-1|domestic|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Number of courses from which the student officially withdrew during the term. Distinct from DFW count — used for financial aid Return to Title IV (R2T4) calculations and enrollment intensity adjustments.
Unique surrogate identifier for the academic term record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the term master table.

Foreign key linking to aid.award_year. Business justification: Every enrollment term belongs to a financial aid award year governing all aid eligibility, packaging, and disbursement for that term. The term.financial_aid_year_code is a denormalized representation

Foreign key linking to facility.campus. Business justification: Academic terms may have campus-specific calendars, registration periods, and census dates in multi-campus institutions. Term management and registration systems require campus assignment. Campus_code

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Academic terms must align with fiscal periods for tuition revenue recognition, financial statement preparation, IPEDS reporting, and audit compliance. Every institution maps academic calendars to fisc

The academic year to which this term belongs, expressed in YYYY-YYYY format (e.g., '2024-2025'). Used for IPEDS reporting, financial aid year alignment, accreditation data submissions, and annual enrollment trend analysis.
reference: IPEDS Glossary; U.S. Department of Education Title IV; SACSCOC Standards

description: The last date students may add courses to their schedule for this term. After this date, course additions require instructor and dean approval. Critical for enrollment management, financial aid recalculation, and DFW rate tracking.
reference: Ellucian Banner Registration Control (SOATERM); U.S. Department of Education

description: The native term code as stored in the Ellucian Banner Student Information System (STVTERM table). Serves as the integration key for data lineage tracing between the Silver Layer and the Banner SIS source system. Enables reconciliation during ETL validation.
reference: Ellucian Banner STVTERM; ETL Integration Standards

description: The institutional calendar structure under which this term operates. Determines credit hour equivalencies, ECTS conversion factors, and financial aid disbursement schedules. Reported to IPEDS and regional accreditors.
reference: IPEDS Glossary; ECTS User Guide; U.S. Department of Education

description: The official enrollment snapshot date used for IPEDS headcount and FTE reporting, state funding calculations, and institutional enrollment benchmarking. Enrollment counts frozen at census are the authoritative figures for accreditation and regulatory submissions.
reference: IPEDS Fall Enrollment Survey; U.S. Department of Education; NACUBO Reporting Standards

description: The date of the graduation ceremony associated with this term, if applicable. Used for degree conferral processing, alumni record creation in Blackbaud Raiser's Edge, and NCAA eligibility tracking for student-athletes.
reference: Ellucian Banner Graduation; Blackbaud Raiser's Edge; NCAA Eligibility Center

description: The timestamp when this term record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements, data lineage tracking, and FERPA compliance documentation for record management.
reference: FERPA; NACUBO Data Governance Standards; Databricks Lakehouse Audit Standards

description: The official date on which degrees are conferred for students completing requirements in this term. May differ from commencement_date (e.g., degrees conferred at end of term but ceremony held later). Used for transcript notation, alumni record activation, and IPEDS Completions Survey reporting.
reference: Ellucian Banner Graduation; IPEDS Completions Survey; U.S. Department of Education

description: The last date students may drop courses without academic record notation (no 'W' grade). Drops before this date result in full or partial tuition refund per the institutional refund schedule. Impacts DFW rate calculations and enrollment reporting.
reference: Ellucian Banner Registration Control (SOATERM); U.S. Department of Education Title IV

description: The last day of the academic term when instruction concludes. Marks the boundary for enrollment reporting, financial aid return-to-title-IV (R2T4) calculations, and academic standing evaluations.
reference: Ellucian Banner STVTERM; U.S. Department of Education Title IV R2T4; IPEDS

description: The scheduled date for initial financial aid disbursement to student accounts for this term. Must comply with U.S. Department of Education Title IV disbursement timing rules (no earlier than 10 days before term start for most aid types). Drives student billing and accounts receivable processing.
reference: U.S. Department of Education Title IV; FAFSA Regulations; NACUBO Student Financial Services

description: The minimum number of credit hours required for a student to be classified as full-time for FTE calculation purposes in this term. Typically 12 credit hours for undergraduates and 9 for graduates. Used in IPEDS FTE reporting, state funding formulas, and financial aid full-time enrollment requirements.
reference: IPEDS Glossary; U.S. Department of Education; NACUBO FTE Reporting Standards

description: The last date by which faculty or academic administrators may submit grade changes (corrections or incomplete grade resolutions) for this term without requiring academic committee approval. Governs academic record integrity and transcript finalization.
reference: Ellucian Banner Academic History; FERPA; HLC Accreditation Standards

description: The deadline by which faculty must submit final grades for all courses in this term via the SIS or LMS gradebook. Triggers academic standing evaluation, Dean's List processing, and financial aid SAP review upon completion.
reference: Ellucian Banner Academic History; Canvas LMS Gradebook; HLC Accreditation Standards

description: The minimum number of credit hours required for a student to be classified as half-time enrollment status for this term. Typically 6 credit hours for undergraduates. Determines financial aid eligibility levels and loan deferment qualification under Title IV regulations.
reference: U.S. Department of Education Title IV; FAFSA Regulations; Ellucian Banner Financial Aid

description: The deadline by which students must complete coursework to resolve an 'Incomplete' (I) grade assigned in this term. After this date, incomplete grades typically convert to a failing grade per institutional policy. Tracked in Banner Academic History.
reference: Ellucian Banner Academic History; HLC Accreditation Standards; SACSCOC

description: The number of weeks of scheduled instruction in this term. Used for credit hour compliance verification per U.S. Department of Education regulations (Carnegie Unit standard: 1 credit hour = 1 hour of instruction + 2 hours of out-of-class work per week for 15 weeks), ECTS conversion, and accreditation reporting.
reference: U.S. Department of Education Credit Hour Definition (34 CFR 600.2); ECTS User Guide; HLC

description: Primary instructional delivery modality for this term. Reported to IPEDS and accreditors. 'Hyflex' indicates simultaneous in-person and online delivery. Drives distance education compliance reporting under U.S. Department of Education regulations.
reference: IPEDS Distance Education Definition; U.S. Department of Education; HLC Distance Education Standards

business_glossary_term: Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System (IPEDS) Term Type Code
description: IPEDS-defined term type code for federal enrollment reporting: '1' = Fall, '2' = Spring, '3' = Summer. Required for IPEDS Fall Enrollment Survey and 12-Month Enrollment Survey submissions to the U.S. Department of Education.
reference: IPEDS Data Collection System; U.S. Department of Education IPEDS Glossary

description: Indicates whether credits earned in this term count toward degree completion requirements. Non-degree-applicable terms (e.g., certain continuing education sessions) are excluded from academic progress calculations and transcript degree audits.
reference: Ellucian Banner Academic History; HLC Accreditation Standards; SACSCOC

description: Indicates whether students enrolled in this term are eligible to receive Title IV federal financial aid disbursements. Some special sessions or non-credit terms may not qualify. Governs FAFSA processing and aid packaging in Banner Financial Aid.
reference: U.S. Department of Education Title IV; FAFSA Regulations; Ellucian Banner Financial Aid

description: Indicates whether this term uses open/rolling enrollment (True) rather than a fixed registration window (False). Relevant for MOOC-style programs, continuing education, and competency-based education offerings that do not follow traditional term calendars.
reference: U.S. Department of Education Competency-Based Education Guidelines; HLC

description: Indicates whether this term follows the institution's standard academic calendar (True) or is a non-standard/accelerated/special session term (False). Non-standard terms may have different financial aid disbursement rules and credit hour compliance requirements under Title IV.
reference: U.S. Department of Education Title IV Non-Standard Term Rules; Ellucian Banner

description: The last official day of academic activity for this term, used for Title IV Return to Title IV (R2T4) calculations when a student withdraws. Distinct from end_date as it may exclude final exam periods for R2T4 purposes.
reference: U.S. Department of Education Title IV R2T4 Regulations; Ellucian Banner

description: The deadline by which students must pay tuition and fees or establish a payment plan to avoid late fees or enrollment cancellation for this term. Managed in Banner Student Accounts.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Accounts; NACUBO Student Financial Services Standards

description: The final date by which students may register for courses in this term through standard registration processes. After this date, late registration or departmental override processes apply.
reference: Ellucian Banner Registration Control (SOATERM); HLC Accreditation Standards

description: The date on which student course registration opens for this term. Priority registration windows for different student populations (e.g., seniors, athletes, honors students) are governed by this baseline date in Banner's registration control.

The date on which Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) is evaluated for students enrolled in this term. SAP evaluation is required by U.S. Department of Education Title IV regulations to determine continued financial aid eligibility based on GPA and completion rate thresholds.
reference: U.S. Department of Education Title IV SAP Regulations (34 CFR 668.34); Ellucian Banner Financial Aid

description: Sub-term session identifier used when a term contains multiple instructional sessions of different lengths (e.g., 'A', 'B', 'C' for 8-week sessions within a 16-week semester, or 'S1'/'S2' for summer sessions). Aligns with Banner part-of-term configuration.
reference: Ellucian Banner Part-of-Term (SOATERM); U.S. Department of Education

description: The first day of the academic term when instruction begins. Used to calculate enrollment duration, financial aid disbursement eligibility, and Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) evaluation periods.
reference: Ellucian Banner STVTERM; U.S. Department of Education Title IV; IPEDS

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the academic term, following Ellucian Banner's YYYYTT convention (e.g., 202410 for Fall 2024, 202420 for Spring 2025). Used as the primary cross-system integration key across Banner, Canvas LMS, and Kuali Research.
reference: Ellucian Banner STVTERM; IPEDS Reporting Standards

description: Human-readable display name of the academic term (e.g., 'Fall 2024', 'Spring 2025', 'Summer 2024 Session I'). Used in student-facing portals, transcripts, and institutional reporting.
reference: Ellucian Banner STVTERM; IPEDS Reporting Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the academic term. 'Planning' indicates the term is being configured; 'Open' indicates registration is available; 'Active' indicates the term is in session; 'Closed' indicates the term has ended and grades are finalized; 'Archived' indicates the term is historical. Controls system-wide access and processing rules in Banner.
reference: Ellucian Banner Term Control Table; HLC Accreditation Standards

description: Classification of the academic term by season or session type. Drives financial aid disbursement cycles, IPEDS reporting, and enrollment analytics segmentation.
reference: IPEDS Glossary; U.S. Department of Education Title IV Regulations

description: The date on which tuition and fee charges are assessed to student accounts for this term. Aligns with the institutional billing cycle and triggers accounts receivable processing in Banner Student Accounts and PeopleSoft Financials.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Accounts; Oracle PeopleSoft Financials AR; NACUBO

description: The timestamp when this term record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Silver Layer.
reference: FERPA; NACUBO Data Governance Standards; Databricks Lakehouse Audit Standards

description: The last date students may withdraw from courses with a 'W' grade notation on their academic record. Withdrawals after this date require academic committee approval. Affects SAP evaluation, financial aid return-to-title-IV (R2T4), and DFW rate reporting.
reference: Ellucian Banner Registration Control (SOATERM); U.S. Department of Education Title IV R2T4; SACSCOC

description: Unique identifier for each academic history record. Primary key for the academic history product.
Unique identifier for the course taken. Links to the course catalog master record.
Identifier for the specific section of the course in which the student was enrolled.

Foreign key linking to instruction.final_grade. Business justification: Grade posting reconciliation and transcript audit require linking each academic_history record to the authoritative final_grade record it was built from. Registrars use this to verify posted transcrip

Foreign key linking to instruction.grade_entry. Business justification: Transcript integrity audits and grade appeal processes require tracing each academic_history record back to the specific gradebook grade_entry that sourced it. Higher-ed registrars and academic integr
Unique identifier for the instructor of record who taught this section. Links to the faculty master record.
reference: CUPA-HR (College and University Professional Association for Human Resources)

description: Unique identifier for the student to whom this academic history record belongs. Links to the student master record.
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act)

description: Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_registration. Business justification: Grade change audit trails and transcript verification require tracing each academic_history record back to the originating enrollment_registration. Registrars processing grade changes and auditors ver

Foreign key linking to research.research_award. Business justification: Federal effort reporting (2 CFR 200) requires linking student RA academic activity to the specific funded award. Graduate research courses and RA appointments must be traceable to the sponsoring award

Foreign key linking to studentlife.service_learning_placement. Business justification: Academic history records for service learning credit must reference the originating service_learning_placement for transcript accuracy and accreditation reporting. Registrars and accreditation officer

Foreign key linking to enrollment.student_term_record. Business justification: GPA recalculation, SAP audits, and transcript verification require aggregating course-level academic_history records into the term-level student_term_record. Registrars and financial aid offices need
Identifier for the academic term in which the course was taken (e.g., Fall 2023, Spring 2024).

Foreign key linking to student.transfer_credit. Business justification: When transfer credits are accepted and posted to a student's record, they appear in academic_history with transfer_credit_indicator=true. Linking academic_history to the originating transfer_credit re
The academic level at which the course was taken (e.g., undergraduate, graduate, professional, doctoral).
Indicates the impact of this course result on the student's academic standing (e.g., positive contribution, neutral, negative impact, triggered probation or dismissal).
The completion status of the course indicating whether the student successfully completed the course requirements.
The instructional delivery method for the course (e.g., in-person, online, hybrid, blended, synchronous, asynchronous).
The numeric level of the course indicating its difficulty or year level (e.g., 100-level for freshman, 200-level for sophomore, 500-level for graduate).
The unique course reference number (CRN) assigned to the section for registration purposes. Commonly used in Ellucian Banner systems.
The official title of the course as it appears on the transcript (e.g., Introduction to Psychology, Calculus I).
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act)

description: The number of credit hours the student attempted for this course. Used in GPA (Grade Point Average) and SAP (Satisfactory Academic Progress) calculations.
The number of credit hours the student successfully earned for this course. May differ from attempted hours if the course was failed or withdrawn.
Flag indicating whether this course counts toward the student's degree requirements for their declared program.
Flag indicating whether this course is eligible for financial aid consideration under Title IV regulations.
reference: Title IV Federal Student Aid Regulations

description: Flag indicating whether this course fulfills a general education or core curriculum requirement.
Flag indicating whether this course is included in the student's GPA calculation. Some courses (e.g., pass/fail, audit, transfer credits) may be excluded.
The date on which the grade was last changed, if applicable. Used for audit trail and compliance purposes.
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act)

description: Flag indicating whether the grade for this course has been changed after initial posting. Used for audit trail purposes.
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act)

description: The documented reason for any grade change, required for audit trail and FERPA compliance.
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act)

description: The letter grade or grade code earned by the student for this course (e.g., A, B+, C-, F, W for withdrawal, I for incomplete, P for pass, NP for no pass, AU for audit).
The total grade points earned for this course, calculated as credit hours multiplied by the grade point value (e.g., 4.0 for A, 3.0 for B). Used in GPA (Grade Point Average) calculation.
Flag indicating whether this grade replaces a previous grade for GPA calculation purposes under the institution's grade replacement policy.
The grading basis under which the student took the course (e.g., standard letter grade, pass/fail, audit, credit/no credit).
Flag indicating whether this course was taken as part of an honors program or honors section.
Flag indicating whether this course fulfills a major requirement for the student's declared major program.
Flag indicating whether this course was delivered entirely online or through distance education.
Alternative term for grade points used in some institutions. Represents the numeric value assigned to the grade for GPA calculation purposes.
The timestamp when this academic history record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail purposes.
The timestamp when this academic history record was last updated. Used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.
The current or final registration status for this course enrollment (e.g., registered, dropped, withdrawn, completed).
The number of times the student has attempted this specific course, including the current attempt.
Flag indicating whether this course is a repeat of a previously taken course. True if the student has taken this course before.
The code or identifier for the institution from which transfer credits were earned. Populated only for transfer credits.
The name of the institution from which transfer credits were earned. Populated only for transfer credits.
The subject area code for the course (e.g., MATH, ENGL, BIOL, CHEM).
Flag indicating whether this course should appear on the official transcript. Some courses may be excluded from transcript display per institutional policy.
reference: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act)

description: Flag indicating whether this course was transferred from another institution rather than taken at the home institution.
Unique identifier for the academic standing evaluation record. Primary key for this entity.
Identifier of the staff member, office, or automated system that performed or certified the academic standing evaluation. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Identifier of the academic term for which this standing evaluation was performed. Links to the term reference table.
Unique identifier of the student being evaluated for academic standing. Links to the student master record in the Student Information System (SIS).

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: SAP evaluation is directly mandated by 34 CFR 668.34 (Title IV regulatory requirement). academic_standing has policy_id but no FK to the specific regulatory_requirement. A financial aid compliance off

Foreign key linking to enrollment.student_term_record. Business justification: Academic standing evaluation is driven by the term-level GPA and credit hours in student_term_record. Reconciliation reports and SAP audits require joining the authoritative standing determination (st

Indicates whether the student is required to follow an academic plan as a condition of continued enrollment or financial aid eligibility. Typically required for students on probation or whose appeal was conditionally approved.
The outcome of the student's SAP appeal. Approved appeals may reinstate Title IV eligibility with or without an academic plan. Denied appeals result in loss of federal financial aid eligibility.

business_glossary_term: Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) Appeal Decision Date
description: The date on which the SAP appeal committee rendered a decision on the student's appeal.

business_glossary_term: Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) Appeal Submitted Indicator
description: Indicates whether the student submitted a formal appeal for SAP suspension or probation status. Appeals typically cite extenuating circumstances such as medical issues, family emergencies, or other documented hardships.

The percentage of attempted credit hours that the student has successfully completed (earned credits / attempted credits * 100). Federal SAP requires a minimum 67% completion rate (pace) for Title IV eligibility.
reference: 34 CFR 668.34 - Title IV SAP Pace Requirement

description: The date and time when this academic standing evaluation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Number of credit hours the student is currently enrolled in for the upcoming or current term. Used for enrollment status verification.
Total number of credit hours the student has attempted across all terms at the institution, including courses in progress, completed, withdrawn, and failed. Used in SAP pace calculation.
Total number of credit hours the student has successfully completed with a passing grade across all terms at the institution. Used in SAP pace calculation and degree progress tracking.
The student's cumulative grade point average across all terms at the institution, calculated on a 4.0 scale. Includes all graded coursework attempted at the institution, excluding transfer credits.
The date on which the academic standing evaluation was performed. Typically occurs at the end of each term after final grades are posted.
Free-text notes or comments recorded by the evaluator regarding special circumstances, policy exceptions, or additional context for the standing determination. Confidential institutional record.

business_glossary_term: Grade Point Average (GPA) Threshold Met Indicator
description: Indicates whether the student met the minimum cumulative GPA threshold required for satisfactory academic progress. Threshold varies by academic level and institutional policy.

The official academic standing status assigned by the institution based on cumulative and term GPA performance. Determines eligibility for continued enrollment, honors recognition, and academic intervention programs.

business_glossary_term: Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System (IPEDS) Reportable Indicator
description: Indicates whether this academic standing record should be included in IPEDS reporting cohorts for retention, graduation rate, and academic progress metrics.
Indicates whether the student has exceeded the maximum timeframe allowed to complete their degree program. Federal SAP requires completion within 150% of the published program length.
reference: 34 CFR 668.34 - Title IV SAP Maximum Timeframe

description: The date on which the student was notified of their academic standing evaluation results. Important for compliance with institutional notification requirements and appeal deadlines.
Indicates whether the student was officially notified of their academic standing status and any associated actions required (probation, suspension, appeal rights, etc.).
Indicates whether the student met the minimum completion rate (pace) threshold required for satisfactory academic progress. Federal requirement is 67% for Title IV eligibility.
reference: 34 CFR 668.34 - Title IV SAP Pace Requirement

description: The version identifier of the academic standing and SAP policy that was applied during this evaluation. Ensures historical evaluations can be audited against the policy in effect at the time.

Federal Title IV financial aid eligibility status based on Satisfactory Academic Progress evaluation. Determines whether the student meets federal requirements for GPA, pace of completion, and maximum timeframe to continue receiving federal financial aid.
Number of credit hours the student attempted during the evaluation term, including completed, withdrawn, and failed courses.
Number of credit hours the student successfully completed with a passing grade during the evaluation term.
The student's grade point average for the specific evaluation term, calculated on a 4.0 scale. Used to assess term-level academic performance.

business_glossary_term: Title IV Financial Aid Eligible Indicator
description: Indicates whether the student is eligible to receive federal Title IV financial aid based on the SAP evaluation results. False if SAP status is suspension or if appeal was denied.
The grade point average from transfer coursework accepted by the institution, if applicable. May be used in combined GPA calculations depending on institutional policy.
The date and time when this academic standing evaluation record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

Unique identifier for the degree progress record. Primary key. Inferred role: MASTER_AGREEMENT (degree progress represents a student's binding relationship with a declared academic program at a point in time).
Unique identifier for the declared academic program (degree, major, concentration). Links to the curriculum domain for degree requirements.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Academic Program

description: Foreign key linking to curriculum.concentration. Business justification: Degree audit and graduation clearance require linking a student's declared concentration to the official curriculum.concentration record. Advisors and registrars use this to verify concentration requi

Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Degree audit and progress tracking are governed by institutional degree requirements policy (catalog year policy, substitution/waiver policy). degree_progress has accreditation_review_id but no FK to
Unique identifier for the student whose degree progress is being tracked. Links to the student master record.

Foreign key linking to enrollment.student_term_record. Business justification: Degree audit systems evaluate degree_progress against the per-term academic snapshot in student_term_record to compute completion percentage and verify SAP status. Academic advisors and degree audit e
The academic term in which the student is expected to complete all degree requirements and graduate (e.g., Spring 2025).
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Graduation Planning

description: Free-text notes or comments from the degree audit system regarding exceptions, substitutions, or special conditions affecting degree progress.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Degree Audit

description: The academic catalog year under which the student is pursuing the degree (e.g., 2023-2024). Determines the set of degree requirements applicable to the student.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Catalog

description: The six-digit CIP code that classifies the instructional program according to the National Center for Education Statistics taxonomy (e.g., 11.0701 for Computer Science).
Percentage of degree requirements completed, calculated as (credits completed + transferred + waived) / total credits required * 100.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Degree Audit

description: Total number of credit hours the student has successfully completed toward the degree program, including passed courses with grades of D or better (or institution-specific passing threshold).
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Academic History

description: Total number of credit hours the student is currently enrolled in for the current term that count toward degree requirements.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Registration

description: Total number of credit hours still required to complete the degree program, calculated as total required minus completed, transferred, and waived credits.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Degree Audit

description: Total number of credit hours accepted from other institutions or prior learning assessments that count toward the degree program.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Transfer Credit

description: Total number of credit hours waived due to exemptions, advanced placement, or other institutional policies.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Degree Audit

description: The date on which the student officially declared this major or program.
The timestamp when the most recent degree audit was executed to calculate progress toward degree completion.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Degree Audit

description: The level of the degree program the student is pursuing (e.g., Bachelor's, Master's, Doctoral).
The type of degree being pursued (e.g., BA, BS, MA, MS, PhD, MBA).
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Academic Program

description: The date from which this degree progress record is effective. Supports point-in-time tracking of program changes.
Total number of elective credit hours the student has successfully completed.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Degree Audit

description: Total number of elective credit hours required for the degree program.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Degree Requirements

description: The date on which this degree progress record is no longer effective (e.g., when the student changes programs or graduates). Null for current active records.
Total number of general education or core curriculum credit hours the student has successfully completed.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Degree Audit

description: Total number of general education or core curriculum credit hours required for the degree program.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Degree Requirements

description: The institutional code for the student's declared major within the academic program.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Academic Program

description: Total number of credit hours the student has successfully completed in the major field of study.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Degree Audit

description: Total number of credit hours required in the major field of study.
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Degree Requirements

description: The full name of the student's declared major (e.g., Computer Science, Biology, Business Administration).
reference: Ellucian Banner Student Information System - Academic Program

description: The minimum cumulative or program GPA required for degree conferral under the applicable catalog year.

Boolean flag indicating whether the student is enrolled in an institutional honors program for this term.

Timestamp when this enrollment status record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization with downstream systems.

Unique identifier of the student taking the leave of absence. Links to the student master record.

description: Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Leave of absence records carry tuition_refund_eligibility_flag and tuition_refund_percentage. When a refund is issued, the bursar generates a credit AR invoice or adjustment. Linking the leave record
Actual date when the student returned from leave and re-enrolled. Null if student has not yet returned. Used to measure leave duration accuracy and Title IV compliance.
